metadata.filed_by: Shri Anil Verma, Senior Investigating Officer, ACB

content:
I, Shri Anil Verma, Senior Investigating Officer, Anti-Corruption Bureau, Bhopal, do hereby file this First Information Report under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 read with Section 154 CrPC.

On 15th August 2023, at approximately 10:30 AM, a forensic audit report prepared by the State Financial Intelligence Unit (SFIU) was received by this office, indicating disproportionate assets held by Mr. Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation. The audit revealed that over a three-year period (2020–2023), Mr. Sharma’s declared annual income of ₹8.2 lakh is inconsistent with bank transactions totaling ₹47.6 lakh, including three cash deposits of ₹5 lakh each in March, May, and July 2022, and a property purchase of ₹2.1 crore in Indore under the name of his wife, Mrs. Anjali Sharma, on 15th June 2022.

The property purchase lacks any documented source of income, and no inheritance records from Mr. Ramesh Sharma (deceased father) have been produced despite claims of inheritance. Additionally, encrypted WhatsApp communications between Mr. Sharma and real estate broker Vikram Singh contain references to "commission" and "paperwork clearance" coinciding with the approval of three irregular land regularization cases.

An anonymous complaint was received on 20th July 2023 from Ms. Priya Mehta, a junior clerk at Bhopal Municipal Corporation, who claimed firsthand knowledge of cash bribes being exchanged. Based on this evidence, a raid was conducted at Mr. Sharma’s residence on 15th August 2023, during which bank statements, property documents, mobile phones, and a fabricated inheritance deed dated 2019 were seized.

The accused has been summoned for questioning. This case involves criminal misconduct under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, and a full investigation is underway.

Signed,
Shri Anil Verma
Senior Investigating Officer
Anti-Corruption Bureau, Bhopal
